message.content:
I need you to research the git history of this repository to find significant Recipe Bottle milestones between July 2025 and March 2026, for updating a project timeline.

**CRITICAL FILTERING**: The git log contains many Job Jockey (JJ) bookkeeping commits that are NOT milestones. Only look at commits with `:n:` (notch) or `:W:` (wrap) suffixes — these are actual work commits. Skip ALL commits with these suffixes: `:T:` (slate), `:d:` (paddock), `:f:` (furlough), `:S:` (enroll), `:i:` (OFFICIUM). Also skip any `vvb:` prefixed commits.

Run these commands to get the actual work commits with dates:

```
git log --format="%ad %h %s" --date=short --since="2025-07-28" --until="2026-03-15" --reverse | grep -E ':[nW]:' | head -100
```

Also get a broader view:
```
git log --format="%ad %h %s" --date=short --since="2025-07-28" --until="2026-03-15" --reverse | grep -E ':[nW]:' | wc -l
```

From the work commits, identify **major milestones** — not individual bug fixes or small tweaks, but significant capability additions, infrastructure changes, or architectural shifts. I need ACCURATE dates from the git log (the `--date=short` format gives YYYY-MM-DD).

Group them into timeline entries suitable for an open-source project's "Significant Events" section. Think about what would matter to an outside observer: new capabilities, architectural changes, quality gates, consumer-facing artifacts.

Return: a proposed list of 5-8 timeline entries with exact dates (or month if a milestone spans multiple commits), a one-line description, and a brief note on why it matters.

content.text: I'll run the git log commands to get the work commits and their count.

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire timeline. Let me synthesize the major milestones.

Based on thorough analysis of ~3,000 commits (1,505 pre-JJ format + 1,520 JJ-era work commits) spanning July 28, 2025 through March 14, 2026, here are the proposed timeline entries:

---

**Proposed Timeline: Significant Events (July 2025 - March 2026)**

**1. 2025-08-05 -- Migration from GitHub Actions/GHCR to Google Cloud Platform**
- Date anchored to commit `527239f7`: "TECTONIC CHANGE. It is time to stop with the Github Actions. GHCR isn't safe and its clearly good money after bad now."
- Description: Abandoned GitHub Actions and GHCR for container builds and registry; began migration to Google Cloud Build and Artifact Registry.
- Why it matters: Foundational infrastructure decision -- the entire supply chain security model (service accounts, OAuth, IAM role separation) stems from this pivot. The subsequent weeks (Aug 5-30) established the Governor/Payor/Director/Retriever role architecture that remains the security backbone.

**2. 2025-08-23 -- First Google Cloud Build project stood up with role-based service accounts**
- Date range: Aug 23-29, anchored to commit `c6a3bf5a`: "NEW PROJECT CREATED" and subsequent SA lifecycle work.
- Description: First successful GCP project provisioned with Governor, Director, Mason, and Retriever service accounts; OAuth-based Payor authentication operational.
- Why it matters: Proved out the multi-role IAM architecture where no single credential has full access -- the Payor (human) delegates to machine roles with scoped permissions.

**3. 2025-12-29 to 2025-12-31 -- Bash container runtime operational (Docker bottle/sentry architecture)**
- Date range spans multiple heats: "dockerize-bashize-proto-bottle" and "cloud-first-light."
- Description: Migrated container orchestration from Makefile to pure bash. Implemented sentry/bottle network isolation, iptables firewall, DNS control, nsproto security tests (22 passing), and OCI Layout Bridge for multi-platform builds.
- Why it matters: First end-to-end working system -- from Cloud Build producing images through local container startup with network security enforcement. OCI Layout Bridge solved the buildx-to-GAR authentication problem that blocked multi-platform builds.

**4. 2026-01-04 to 2026-01-13 -- Job Jockey initiative tracker rewritten in Rust**
- Description: Replaced bash-based Job Jockey (heat/pace tracking) with a Rust backend compiled as `vvx jjx` subcommands. 133+ tests, Gallops data model, steeplechase (git commit audit trail), and slash command interface for Claude Code.
- Why it matters: The project management system that all subsequent development used was itself built during this period. Enabled structured multi-heat development with machine-readable commit discipline.

**5. 2026-01-16 to 2026-01-18 -- Voce Viva Kit (VVK) release system with hallmark versioning**
- Description: Implemented kit release/install/uninstall infrastructure (`vvx_emplace`, `vvx_vacate`), hallmark version tracking (brand 1000+), managed CLAUDE.md sections with template freshen/collapse, and multi-kit registry. First validated MVP round-trip on external project.
- Why it matters: Created the distribution mechanism for Claude Code tooling kits -- the infrastructure that allows JJK, VOK, and other kits to be installed into any project with version tracking and clean uninstall.

**6. 2026-03-02 to 2026-03-03 -- Cloud Build v2 migration (Developer Connect to GitLab)**
- Date anchored to depot lifecycle rewrite commits and GitLab connection work.
- Description: Migrated from Google Developer Connect (GitHub-only) to Cloud Build v2 connections with GitLab as the rubric repository host. Rewrote `depot_create`, `depot_destroy`, deleted `depot_initialize`, implemented Secret Manager PAT retrieval, connection-creation retry with zombie cleanup.
- Why it matters: Decoupled the build pipeline from GitHub, enabling GitLab (or any Git host) as the source-of-truth for build definitions. Eliminated the Developer Connect single-point-of-failure and the need for embedded credentials.

**7. 2026-03-05 -- SLSA Build Level 3 provenance verified end-to-end**
- Date anchored to commits `c4a59351` (3 single-arch vessels verified) and `43ae142a` (multi-platform Level 3 validated).
- Description: Achieved and verified SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 on all build pipelines -- both single-architecture and multi-platform. Dual provenance signatures (v0.1 and v1), per-platform attestation with shared `buildInvocationId`, manifest reassembly preserving provenance chain.
- Why it matters: The core supply chain integrity claim -- every container image has cryptographically verifiable build provenance. This is the security property that makes Recipe Bottle's "don't trust the author" model credible.

**8. 2026-03-12 to 2026-03-14 -- Consumer onboarding guide validated end-to-end; bind/graft vessel modes; publication preparation**
- Description: Full onboarding walkthrough from blank configuration to running bottle validated (9-step guide across Payor/Governor/Director/Retriever roles). Added bind vessel mode (upstream image mirroring with digest fidelity verification) and graft vessel mode spec. Created consumer README and CLAUDE.md. BCG compliance audit across 19 files. Architecture diagram added. Security review invitation published.
- Why it matters: Transition from "it works for the author" to "someone else can set this up." The bind mode means Recipe Bottle can consume any upstream image with the same vouch/provenance/SBOM pipeline used for custom builds. The consumer docs and security review invitation signal readiness for external evaluation.
